qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unquoted SSH stream-upload path allows command injection via file_name
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory fixes an insecure sudoers documentation example in docs/index.md, while this finding targets runtime shell interpolation in proxmox/ssh/client.go uploadFile.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSH client auto-trusts unknown host keys and authenticates to attacker-controlled nodes
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is a documentation-level wildcard tee path traversal in sudoers guidance; this finding instead concerns SSH trust-on-first-use behavior in proxmox/ssh/client.go openNodeShell.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's documented sudoers root cause: a wildcard tee path in docs/index.md that permits ../ traversal to unintended files.
evidence: The advisory JSON describes an insecure documentation recommendation: terraform ALL=(root) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/tee /var/lib/vz/*, with ../../../ traversal to files like /etc/sudoers.d/sudo.

The referenced fix commit bd604c41a31e2a55dd6acc01b0608be3ea49c023 patches docs/index.md by replacing wildcard tee rules with snippets-only filename patterns and adding a security warning; it does not patch proxmox/ssh/client.go or other runtime SSH code.

ssh_stream_upload_command_injection targets unquoted remoteFilePath interpolation in proxmox/ssh/client.go uploadFile, which is a different sink and root cause than the advisory's sudoers documentation bug.
ssh_unknown_host_auto_trust targets permissive unknown-host acceptance in proxmox/ssh/client.go openNodeShell, which is unrelated to the advisory's documented tee wildcard path traversal.
